# Test cmd2mcpserver dry-run functionality
#
# This test verifies that cmd2mcpserver can output txtar format
# when using the -dry-run flag

# Create a test program in the workspace
-- testprog/main.go --
package main

import (
	"flag"
	"fmt"
)

var name = flag.String("name", "world", "name to greet")
var verbose = flag.Bool("verbose", false, "enable verbose output")

func main() {
	flag.Parse()
	if *verbose {
		fmt.Printf("Greeting %s\n", *name)
	}
	fmt.Printf("Hello, %s!\n", *name)
}

-- testprog/go.mod --
module github.com/test/testprog

go 1.21

# Run cmd2mcpserver with dry-run flag
exec ../../cmd2mcpserver -dry-run testprog
stdout "-- main.go --"
stdout "-- go.mod --"
stdout "-- README.md --"

# Check for expected content in the output
stdout "modelcontextprotocol"
stdout "testprog"
stdout "MCP server"

# Verify flag definitions are detected and included
stdout '"name"'
stdout '"verbose"'

# Check that the generated code returns stdout, stderr, and returnCode
stdout '"stdout":\s*stdout.String\(\)'
stdout '"stderr":\s*stderr.String\(\)'
stdout '"returnCode":\s*0'
stdout 'var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer'
! stderr "error"
